nativeRange.habitat: Found on mesophotic reef slopes at depths of roughly 30 to 60 m (though most commonly encountered shallower than 40 m), with reports from rubble-covered bottoms near the upper edges of vertical drop-offs and from coastal reefs off Borneo.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical Western Pacific reef slope, mesophotic zone

decorPreferences: live rock with arches, caves and ledges for retreats

diet.notes: Documented as a zooplanktivore in the wild, foraging above rubble and reef slope. In captivity, generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped meaty seafood, and high-quality marine pellets or flake, with nori or algae sheets offered for variety. Multiple small feedings per day are commonly advised, especially for new arrivals; copepod/amphipod-rich live rock and a connected refugium tend to help individuals settle in.

summary: A small, mesophotic Western Pacific fairy wrasse with a bronzy-brown body and vivid courtship flashes; generally peaceful, reef-safe, and well suited to mature medium-sized reef aquariums with a secure lid.
careNotes: Cirrhilabrus brunneus was described by G. R. Allen in 2006 from coastal Borneo and is reported from rubble bottoms and reef slopes in the 30–60 m range (usually shallower than 40 m), with most aquarium specimens collected from Indonesia and the Philippines. Generally considered reef-safe and peaceful, but like other Cirrhilabrus, males have been reported to be aggressive toward other male fairy wrasses, dottybacks, basslets, and longfins, so plan stocking accordingly. Best kept as a single individual, as a male/female pair, or as a one-male harem (1M:2–3F) in tanks of 4 ft (~120 cm) or longer; multiple specimens should be introduced simultaneously to avoid conflict. Cirrhilabrus species are protogynous (female-first sequential hermaphrodites); the dominant fish in a group of females tends to transition to male over time. The Brunneus Fairy Wrasse has been reported to be a strong jumper that may bolt upward when startled, particularly at lights-on and lights-off transitions — a tightly fitted lid or eurobrace is strongly advised. Though sometimes listed under the name 'Dusky Wrasse', it is unrelated to the similarly nicknamed Halichoeres marginatus.
breedingNotes: The species is oviparous, with distinct pairing during spawning, typical of the Labridae family. Captive spawning has not been reported in the aquarium hobby literature; replicating a one-male harem in a large reef system may encourage nuptial display behavior.

primary.name: Top Shelf Aquatics — Dusky Brunneus Fairy Wrasse care guide
primary.notes: Primary LiveAquaria product page returned HTTP 404 (https://www.liveaquaria.com/products/brunneus-fairy-wrasse and search fallback yielded no species page); promoted Top Shelf Aquatics care guide to primary. Used for: tank minimum 50–55 US gal (~190–210 L), minimum 3 ft (~90 cm) footprint, temp 72–78 °F (22.2–25.6 °C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, care level 'Easy', peaceful temperament, reef-safe, carnivore diet (pellets/mysis/finely chopped seafood), strong jumping risk (bolts at lights on/off), protogynous sex change in groups of females, harem composition 1M:2–3F in tanks ≥ 4 ft / 120 cm, simultaneous introduction of multiple specimens, max size ~3 in (~7.6 cm).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed max length 4.4 cm SL (male), family Labridae, order Eupercaria, distribution Western Pacific (Indonesia), depth range 30–60 m (usually to 40 m), preferred temperature 27.1–28.3 °C (mean 27.8 °C — averaged with hobby ranges to 24–27 °C), trophic level 3.2 ± 0.4 (zooplanktivore/carnivore), oviparous with distinct pairing, IUCN: Data Deficient (assessed 2009). FishBase did not state sex-change mode explicitly; genus-wide protogyny confirmed by Top Shelf Aquatics.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com — Brunneus Fairy Wrasse
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank min 50 US gal (~190 L), temperature 76–82 °F (24.4–27.8 °C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, care level 'Moderate' (averaged with Top Shelf 'Easy' to intermediate), lifespan 5–7+ years, peaceful temperament, reef-safe, carnivore diet, pairs OK but not mixed with other wrasse species, jumping risk, sexual dimorphism (males more vivid with longer dorsal fin extensions). Listed adult size as 4–5 in but page also shows max 4 in, treated as inconsistent; sided with FishBase/Wikipedia 4.4 cm SL and a 4.4–7.5 cm total-length bracket.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Cirrhilabrus brunneus
additional.notes: Taxonomy/description corroboration: described by G. R. Allen 2006 from coasts off Borneo, depth 40–50 m, family Labridae, max length 4.4 cm; meristics (11 dorsal spines, 9 dorsal soft rays, 3 anal spines, 9 anal soft rays, 15 pectoral rays). IUCN Data Deficient.

additional.name: LiveAquaria (primary; failed)
additional.notes: Designated primary source URL returned HTTP 404; LiveAquaria search-keyword fallback page returned navigation chrome only with no species listing. Verification swapped to Top Shelf Aquatics, FishBase, SaltwaterFish.com, and Wikipedia per playbook fallback rule.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing during spawning, typical of Labridae. Captive spawning has not been reported in the aquarium hobby literature. Protogynous hermaphrodite typical of Cirrhilabrus.
